title: puccinellia capillaris subsp. pulvinata
intro: Puccinellia capillaris subsp. pulvinata, also known as the sea grass or saltwort, is a species of flowering plant in the grass family Poaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Puccinellia capillaris subsp. pulvinata include reducing inflammation, soothing skin irritations, and promoting wound healing. The plant is typically prepared as a tea or infusion, or used as a topical application, with some herbal preparations also utilizing it in capsule or tincture form.
